hypothesis: Integrating a stochastic fabrication-variability model (thickness perturbations, surface roughness, and interfacial diffusion) into CHROMA’s differentiable verifier and training the decoder with a variability-aware loss will produce inverse-designed stacks whose perceptual color fidelity (∆E) remains below the JND threshold across a realistic range of manufacturing tolerances and viewing angles, outperforming designs optimized for nominal geometry.
